prerequisites: A Shopify store, identified by its myshopify.com domain (for example my-shop.myshopify.com). The store subdomain is supplied in the required `shop` config field.
Permission to create a custom app in the Shopify admin, or a registered OAuth app in the Shopify Dev Dashboard, so you can obtain Admin API credentials.

The Admin API access scopes the connector needs (for example read access to orders, products, customers, transactions, and fulfillment orders) granted to the app, since Shopify only returns data for scopes the merchant approved.
Optionally, a start_date and lookback/bulk window values if you want to bound the initial sync; these map to the `start_date`, `lookback_window_in_days`, and `bulk_window_in_days` config fields.

authMethods.name: Custom app Admin API access token (api_password / access_token)
authMethods.summary: Create a custom app from the Shopify admin, configure the required Admin API access scopes, and install it on the store. Installation issues an Admin API access token (the shpat_ token), which Shopify uses to authenticate Admin API requests. Provide it through the `credentials.access_token` field (legacy private apps expose the same value as `credentials.api_password`). This is the simplest option for syncing a single store.

authMethods.name: OAuth app (client_id, client_secret, access_token)
authMethods.summary: Register an app in the Shopify Dev Dashboard to obtain a client ID (API key) and client secret (API secret key). Run Shopify's OAuth authorization code grant: redirect the merchant to the shop's authorization URL with the requested scopes, let them approve, then exchange the returned authorization code for an Admin API access token using the client ID and client secret. Supply `credentials.client_id`, `credentials.client_secret`, and the resulting `credentials.access_token`.

setupSteps.title: Identify your shop domain
setupSteps.body: Determine your store's myshopify.com domain. Enter the store subdomain in the required `shop` config field (for example, for my-shop.myshopify.com use my-shop or the full myshopify.com domain as the connector expects).

setupSteps.title: Create credentials in Shopify
setupSteps.body: For a single store, create a custom app in the Shopify admin, add the Admin API access scopes the connector needs, and install it to generate the Admin API access token. For a distributed/OAuth integration, register an app in the Shopify Dev Dashboard to get a client ID and client secret, then run the OAuth authorization code grant against the shop to obtain an access token.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Populate the `credentials` object. For the custom-app path, set `credentials.access_token` (or `credentials.api_password` for legacy private apps). For the OAuth path, set `credentials.client_id`, `credentials.client_secret`, and `credentials.access_token`. These are the connector's secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional sync settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally tune the non-secret config fields: set `start_date` to bound the initial sync, adjust `lookback_window_in_days` and `bulk_window_in_days` for incremental and bulk extraction windows, and set behavior flags such as `fetch_transactions_user_id`, `fulfillment_orders_include_closed`, `job_product_variants_include_pres_prices`, `job_checkpoint_interval`, and `job_termination_threshold` as needed. All of these are optional.
